title: Three Genes Are Behind Three-Quarters of All Preventable Drug Harm. We've Known for Years.
description: A 1.3-million-report analysis of UK adverse drug reactions found that 75% of all genetically preventable harm traces back to just three genes — and a test that could flag them costs less than a single hospital admission. So why is your doctor still guessing? The story of pharmacogenomics is not about the future of medicine. It's about a failure happening right now.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Three Genes Are Behind Three-Quarters of All Preventable Drug Harm. We've Known for Years.
